So or very. Example: 너무 감사해요 = Thank you: Really or for real. Example: 진짜요? = Really?
Together. Example: 같이 찍어요 = Let's take a photo together.
Still or not yet. Example: 아직 많이 부족해요 = I still have a lot to learn.
Hard or diligently. Example: 열심히 할게요 = I will try hard.
Use for polite present speech. Example: 감사해요 = Thank you.
Use for I will or a soft promise. Example: 열심히 할게요 = I will try hard.
OMG! Amazing! Situation: A fan recognizes you on the street.
Really? Thank you so much. Situation: A fan says they love your content.
No no, I am shy. Situation: A fan compliments your appearance.
I still have a lot to learn. Situation: A fan says your Korean is great.
Let's take a photo together. Situation: You want to take a photo with a fan.
One, two, three. Situation: Taking the selfie countdown.
Aww, no no. Situation: Someone flatters you too much.

너무 (neo-mu): 아 진짜 너무 감사해요 (ah jin-jja neo-mu gam-sa-hae-yo)
So or very. Example: 너무 감사해요 = Thank you: Ah really, thank you so much. Use when: Overwhelmed by kindness.
OMG wow really? Use when: Maximum surprise.
You are so pretty. Use when: Complimenting a fan back.
Thank you so much. Use when: Saying goodbye to a fan warmly.
To be shy or embarrassed. Example: 부끄러워요 = I am shy.
Photo or picture. Example: 사진 찍어요 = Take a photo.
